title: Implement `dateTime64ToUUIDv7` Function
description:
### Company or project name
Target
### Use case
Lots of context here: https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/84244
We would like to increase the precision of the timestamps we are storing and also be able to generate a `UUIDv7` from said timestamps for the same reasons above.
### Describe the solution you'd like
This is more nuanced than the `dateTimeToUUIDv7` implementation. The `UUIDv7` [spec](https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-peabody-dispatch-new-uuid-format-01.html#section-4.4) allocates:
-  `unixts`: 36 bits for the unix timestamp
- `subsec_a`: additional 12 bits for sub-second precision
- `subsec_b`: another 12 bits for even more precision
- `subsec_seq_node`: 62 bits which MAY be allocated to any combination of additional sub-section precision, sequence counter, or pseudo-random data
ClickHouse's current `UUIDv7` implementation uses:
- 48 bits of precision for storing the timestamp (`unixts + subseq_a`)
- 12 bits as a counter for incrementing the uuid when derived from the same timestamp (`subseq_b`).
We could support the full 64 bit precision of `DateTime64`... but we would not be able to use the same bit positions for a counter and still maintain well-ordered UUIDs. Changing the interpretation of the UUID type could have implications if users are using different UUID versions derived from different functions.
```SQL
CREATE TABLE example
(
...
ts DateTime64(3, 'UTC'),
id UUID MATERIALIZED dateTime64ToUUIDv7(ts),
...
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY toUint128(id);
```
### Describe alternatives you've considered
_No response_
### Additional context
Please assign to me if deemed a valuable addition! I have already worked on the `dateTimeToUUIDv7` function.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id UUID, ts TIMESTAMP(6) WITH TIME ZONE, value INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, ts, value) VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), '2023-10-01 12:00:00.123456+00', 100);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, ts, value) VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), '2023-10-01 12:00:00.654321+00', 200);
SELECT id, (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ts) * 1000)::bigint || '-' || (ts::text) AS uuid_v7 FROM test_table WHERE value > 150;
CREATE TABLE summary_table AS SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS count FROM test_table GROUP BY id;
